concerns.analysis: The output contains several section titles that do not appear in the source document. Specifically, the headings 'Prompting Overview', 'About Copilot in Outlook', 'Follow Along - Copilot Chat - Optional', and 'Follow Along - Copilot in Outlook' are not found anywhere in the 21-page source PDF. These are invented organizational headers with no basis in the original content.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source document contains no text matching the headings 'Prompting Overview', 'About Copilot in Outlook', or 'Follow Along'.
concerns.output_quote: "title": "Prompting Overview", "title": "About Copilot in Outlook", "title": "Follow Along - Copilot Chat - Optional", "title": "Follow Along - Copilot in Outlook"
concerns.location: Section titles at indices 7, 10, 16, and 19 of the 'sections' array.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistent and broken in several places. While most slide titles are correctly identified as Level 3 headings, several slides (Pages 9, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, and 19) do not contain a heading element within their 'elements' array. Instead, the titles are either stored only as section titles or are missing entirely. concerns.output_quote: The section corresponding to Page 13 is titled 'Slide 13' and contains no heading element or the text 'Draft with Copilot in Outlook'. The section corresponding to Page 17 is titled 'Practice : Draft with Copilot' but contains no heading element in its 'elements' array.
concerns.location: Sections for Slide 9, Slide 12 (Draft with Copilot in Outlook), Slide 13, Slide 14 (Coaching by Copilot in Outlook), and Practice slides 15-19.

concerns.analysis: Multiple meaningful images from the source PDF are missing or broken in the HTML rendering. On page 2, the group photo and the surfing photo are replaced by green placeholder blocks. Significant diagrams on pages 4 (icons) and 5 (annotated prompt) are missing. Critical software screenshots that provide visual context for the instructions on pages 9, 11, 12, 13, and 14 are also missing, represented only by green blocks. concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains meaningful photos (group photo, surfboard photo on page 2), diagrams (annotated prompt on page 5, icons on page 4), and instructional screenshots (pages 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 20).
concerns.output_quote: In the rendered HTML, these elements are absent and replaced by green placeholder blocks (e.g., Slide 2 shows only two of four photos; Slide 4, 5, 9, 11, 12, 13, and 20 show large green blocks instead of content images).

concerns.analysis: The output fails to identify numerous URLs as active link objects. While the email address on Page 2 is correctly formatted with an 'href', several URLs visible in the PDF (e.g., 'https:///www.microsoft365.com/chat' on Page 6 and 'https://outlook.office365.com/' on Page 16) are rendered as plain text strings rather than interactive link objects with 'href' attributes. This results in missing navigational functionality.
